macro_segments.title: Introduction to Daf 16 and Overview of Chapter Two: From Shelo Lishma to Disqualifying Statuses

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks: Omer count, transition from Shelo Lishma to personal status disqualifications
Review of first locus: Kabbalat HaDam rather than Shechita; non-kohen invalidates via Tzad HaShaveh

macro_segments.title: Non-Kohen (Zar): Source for Prohibition and the Tzad HaShaveh Framework

micro_segments.title: Question: How do we know a non-kohen is prohibited from performing Avodah?
Possible source from Veyinnazru and why it cannot be the basis; pivot to explicit verse Vezar lo yikrav
Challenge to Tzad HaShaveh: zar can bring outside Beit HaMikdash unlike Tamei and Baal Mum
Resolution via Onen: introducing Onen and Baal Mum as the Tzad HaShaveh to exclude bamah restriction
Distinction between zar and onen/baal mum; why onen may be muter on bamah; acceptance of common denominator

macro_segments.title: Kal VaChomer from Yoshev (sitting Kohen) and Return to Tzad HaShaveh

micro_segments.title: Rav Mesharshiya's Kal VaChomer: Yoshev (sitting kohen) can eat but invalidates; Zar cannot eat, how much more invalidates
Challenge: Yoshev has unique disqualification (passul le'edus); may be weightier than non-kohen
Defense: Some yoshev is mutar le'edus (Talmid Chacham); cannot bracket Yoshev as purely invalid
Alternative: Make Tzad HaShaveh from Yoshev and one of the other cases to avoid relying solely on Yoshev
Source for Yoshev being mutar: Verse requires standing before God (Lifnei Hashem), not before Bamah
General principle: prohibited status → invalidates; do not narrow via exceptions (Baal Mum, Tamei, Onen, Yoshev all have exceptions but still invalidate)

macro_segments.title: Onen (Mourner): Proof that Onen Invalidates Sacrifice

micro_segments.title: Source from Kohen Gadol: ומן המקדש לא יצא ולא יחלל; inference that regular kohen who stays would invalidate
Rabbi Eliezer's alternative: Hen HaKrivu (story of Nadav and Avihu); debate whether inference is valid
Context: Nadav and Avihu as Onanim; Aaron as Kohen Gadol can bring as Onen; regular kohanim cannot
Gemara's objection to learning from פסוק without explicit inference; preference for Gezeirah Shavah
Dveih Rabbi Ishmael: Kal VaChomer from Baal Mum (can eat) to Onen (cannot eat) to establish Onen invalidates
Challenge: Baal Mum vs. Zar have different characteristics (mum applies to animal and person; zar only to person)
Challenge: Onen has תקנה (temporary status) unlike Zar (permanent); Baal Mum may also be temporary
Tzad HaShaveh of Zar and Baal Mum: both prohibited, both invalidate; apply to Onen
Source for Onen prohibition: Either Kohen Gadol passage or Hen HaKrivu via Gezeirah Shavah
Attempt to use only Kohen Gadol source; objection that it does not explicitly state regular kohen invalidates
Tzad HaShaveh including Tamei: Tamei has exceptions (Tumah Hutra Betzibur) but still invalidates, so Onen also invalidates despite having exceptions

macro_segments.title: Rava's Proposal: Onen Invalidates only by Korban Yachid, not Tzibur

micro_segments.title: Rava's position: Onen only invalidates by private sacrifice (Korban Yachid), not communal (Korban Tzibur)
Kal VaChomer from Tumah: Tumah not permitted by Kohen Gadol with Yachid, but permitted by Hedyot with Tzibur; Onen (more permissive) should be permitted by Hedyot with Tzibur

macro_segments.title: Rava Bar Ahilai's Refutation: Multiple Kal VaChomer Possibilities Undermine Rava's Logic

micro_segments.title: Challenge: If Rava's Kal VaChomer is valid, other Kal VaChomer combinations yield contradictory results
Alternative inference 1: Onen might not be permitted by Kohen Gadol Yachid (opposite of Rava's assumption)
Alternative inference 2: Tumah might be permitted by Kohen Gadol Yachid (opposite of standard assumption)
Alternative inference 3: Tumah might not be permitted by Hedyot Tzibur (opposite of known law)
Conclusion: Without independent source, cannot decide which parameter to fix; accept Pshat (Tumah = Tzibur idea; Onen = Kohen Gadol idea) without forcing grid-based logic

topical_tags.term: Kal VaChomer (a fortiori; light and heavy argument)
Gezeirah Shavah (verbal analogy; equal language in two verses)
Piggul (invalidation due to intention outside proper time or place)
Tumah Hutra Betzibur (impurity permitted for communal offering)
Veyinnazru mikodeshei benei Yisrael (they shall be separated from the holy things)
Min HaMikdash lo yetzei (from the Temple shall he not go out)
